steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes what readers do with Ashbery's poems; the decisive clue is “opaque subject matter” combined with “heated debate among scholars”—both point to difficulty understanding or explaining what the poems mean.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘delegate’: means to assign a task to someone else. Nothing in the passage is about assigning or entrusting the poems to others. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘compose’: means to write or create. The passage is about readers engaging with the poems, not about Ashbery writing them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘interpret’: means to understand or explain meaning. Opaque subject matter and scholarly debate both describe difficulty determining what the poems mean—exactly what “interpret” captures. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘renounce’: means to formally give up or reject. Nothing in the passage suggests the poems are difficult to reject or abandon. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
